title: sempervivum tectorum subsp. montanum
intro:
Sempervivum tectorum subsp. montanum, also known as the Houseleek or Mountain Sempervivum, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family, often grown as a ornamental due to its unique appearance and low-maintenance requirements.

This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds and promote tissue repair. It is believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Sempervivum tectorum subsp. montanum are often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to accelerate the healing process and soothe skin irritations.
